volumeTitle: The Merchant. The Braggart Soldier. The Ghost. The Persian
label: The Persian (Section 1.3.147b - Section 2.2.207b)

sections.label: Section 1.3.147b
sections.translation: You act kindly . Make haste , be off home ; cleverly tutor your daughter beforehand , instruct her cunningly , what she is to say , where she is to declare she was born ,

sections.label: Section 1.3.150
sections.original: qui sibi parentes fuerint , unde surpta sit .
sections.translation: who were her parents , how she was kidnapped . But let her declare that she was born at a distance from Athens ; and let her shed tears when she makes mention of it .

sections.label: Section 1.3.152b
sections.translation: Now won ’ t you hold your tongue ? Three times more artful is she than you would have her be .

sections.label: Section 1.3.154
sections.original: Lepide hercle dicis . sed scin quid facias ? cape
sections.translation: I ’ troth , you say what ’ s excellent . But do you know what you are to do ? Get

sections.label: Section 1.3.155
sections.original: tunicam atque zonam , et chlamydem adferto et causeam ,
sections.translation: a tunic and a girdle , and bring a scarf and a broad-brimmed hat for him to wear who is to sell her to this Procurer —

sections.label: Section 1.3.156b
sections.translation: Well-capital !

sections.label: Section 1.3.157
sections.translation: As though he were a foreigner .

sections.label: Section 1.3.157b
sections.translation: I approve of it —

sections.label: Section 1.3.157c
sections.translation: And do you bring your daughter cleverly drest up after a foreign fashion .

sections.label: Section 1.3.159
sections.translation: Où sont [*] the dresses ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.159b
sections.translation: Borrow them of the chorus-leader [*] .

sections.label: Section 1.3.160
sections.translation: He ought to lend them ; the Aediles [*] have contracted for them to be found .

sections.label: Section 1.3.161
sections.original: Iam faxo hic aderunt . sed ego nihil horunc scio .
sections.translation: I ’ ll have them here just now . But I ’ m to be acquainted with nothing of these matters ?

sections.label: Section 1.3.162
sections.original: Nihil hercle vero . nam ubi ego argentum accepero ,
sections.translation: I ’ faith , nothing , in fact . But , when I ’ ve got the money , do you at once claim her of the Procurer .

sections.label: Section 1.3.164
sections.original: Sibi habeat , si non extemplo ab eo abduxero .—
sections.translation: Let him keep her for himself , if I don ’ t immediately carry her off from him .

sections.label: Section 1.3.165
sections.original: Abi et istuc cura . interibi egó puerum volo
sections.translation: Be off and attend to this . ( Exit SATURIO .) In the meantime , I want to send a boy to my mistress ; that she may be of good courage , and that I shall manage it to-day . I ’ m talking too much at length .

sections.label: Section 2.1.168
sections.original: Satis fúit índoctae , ímmemori , insípienti dicére totiens .
sections.translation: It were enough to tell an untaught , thoughtless , silly girl the same thing so many times over ; really , in fact , I do imagine that I ’ m quite looked upon by you as a blockhead and a country booby .

sections.label: Section 2.1.170
sections.original: quamquam égo vinum bibo , at mándata non cónsuevi simul bíbere una .
sections.translation: Although I do drink wine , still I ’ m not in the habit of swallowing down your commands together with it . I really had fancied that both myself and my ways had now been sufficiently proved by you ; for , as for me , I ’ ve attended you now these five years ; whereas , in that time , a cuckoo even , I do believe , if he had gone to school , could by now have been made to know his letters well ; while , in the meantime , whether speaking or not speaking [*] , you have not made yourself acquainted with my disposition .

sections.label: Section 2.1.175
sections.original: potin út taceas ? potin né moneas ?
sections.translation: Can you not hold your tongue ? Can you not cease advising me ? I remember , and I know , and I understand , and I keep in mind ; i ’ faith , you are in love , poor thing ; on that account your mind ’ s disturbed . I ’ ll cause that that shall be calmed for you .

sections.label: Section 2.1.179
sections.translation: Wretched is the person that ’ s in love .

sections.label: Section 2.1.179b
sections.translation: Good for nothing , indeed , he certainly is ,

sections.label: Section 2.1.180
sections.original: qui níl amat : quíd ei homini ópus vita est ?
sections.translation: who is in love with nothing . What need has that person of life ? I ought to go , that I may prove obedient to my mistress ; that through my aid she may the sooner become a free woman . I ’ ll go meet this Toxilus , however ; his ears I ’ ll stuff with what has been enjoined upon me .

sections.label: Section 2.2.183
sections.original: Sátin haec tibi sunt plána et certa ? sátin haec meministi ét tenes ?
sections.translation: Are these things quite clear and certain to you — do you quite remember and understand them ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.184
sections.translation: Better than you who have instructed me .

sections.label: Section 2.2.184b
sections.translation: Say you so , you whip-rascal ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.185
sections.translation: I really do say so .

sections.label: Section 2.2.185b
sections.translation: What did I say then P

sections.label: Section 2.2.185c
sections.translation: I ’ ll tell it to her all correctly .

sections.label: Section 2.2.186
sections.translation: I ’ faith , you don ’ t know it .

sections.label: Section 2.2.186b
sections.original: Da hercle pignus , ni omnia memini et scio ,
sections.translation: Troth now , lay me a wager that I don ’ t remember and know it all .

sections.label: Section 2.2.187
sections.original: et quidem sí scis tute , quot hodie habeas digitos in manu .
sections.translation: Why , for my part , I ’ ll lay a wager with you on this , whether you know your own self , how many fingers you have this day upon your hand .

sections.label: Section 2.2.188b
sections.translation: Without hesitation — if you are desirous to lose .

sections.label: Section 2.2.189
sections.translation: A fair truce rather let there be .

sections.label: Section 2.2.189b
sections.original: Tum tu igitur sine me ire .
sections.translation: For that reason , then , do you let me go .

sections.label: Section 2.2.189c
sections.translation: I both bid and permit you .

sections.label: Section 2.2.190
sections.original: sed ita volo te ire , ut domí sis , cum ego te esse illi censeam .
sections.translation: But I wish you so to attend to it , that you are back home while I ’ m thinking that you are there .

sections.label: Section 2.2.191
sections.translation: I ’ ll do so .

sections.label: Section 2.2.191b
sections.translation: Whither are you now going ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.191c
sections.original: Domum : uti domi sim , quom illi censeas .
sections.translation: Home ; that I may be at home while you are thinking that I am there .

sections.label: Section 2.2.192
sections.original: Scelus tu pueri es , atque ob istánc rem ego aliqui te peculiabo .
sections.translation: You are a rascal of a boy , and — for this service I ’ ll give you something to add [*] to your savings .

sections.label: Section 2.2.193
sections.original: Scio fide hercle erili ut soleat impudicitia opprobrari
sections.translation: I ’ m aware how want of shame is wont to be imputed to a master ’ s word , and that masters cannot ever be compelled to appear before the judge on account of those promises .

sections.label: Section 2.2.195
sections.translation: Be off now .

sections.label: Section 2.2.195b
sections.translation: I ’ ll give you reason to command me .

sections.label: Section 2.2.195c
sections.translation: But , Paegnium , take you care and give that letter to Lemniselene herself , and tell her what I bade you .

sections.label: Section 2.2.197
sections.original: Cesso ire ego quo missa sum ?
sections.translation: Do I delay to go whither I was sent ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.198
sections.translation: I ’ m off .

sections.label: Section 2.2.198b
sections.original: I sane . ego domum ibo . face rem hanc cum cura geras .
sections.translation: Then do be off ; I ’ ll off home . Take care and manage this business with attention . Fly post haste .

sections.label: Section 2.2.199b
sections.original: Istuc marinus passer per circum solet .
sections.translation: That ’ s what the ostrich [*] is wont to do in the Circus .

sections.label: Section 2.2.200
sections.original: illic hinc abiit intro huc . sed quis haec ést quae med advorsum incedit ?
sections.translation: He ’ s off from here in-doors there . But who ’ s this woman that ’ s coming towards me ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.201
sections.translation: Surely this is Paegnium .

sections.label: Section 2.2.201b
sections.translation: This is Sophoclidisca , the private servant of her to whom I ’ m sent .

sections.label: Section 2.2.202b
sections.original: Nullus esse hodie hóc puero peior perhibetur .
sections.translation: There ’ s not a person this day that ’ s reported to be more artful than this boy . I ’ ll accost him .

sections.label: Section 2.2.203
sections.translation: I must come to a stop .

sections.label: Section 2.2.203c
sections.translation: At this bar [*]

sections.label: Section 2.2.204
sections.original: Paegnium , deliciae pueri , salve . quid agis ? ut vales ?
sections.translation: Paegnium , my charmer of a boy , save you ; how are you ? How do you do ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.205
sections.translation: Sophoclidisca , the Gods will favour me .

sections.label: Section 2.2.205b
sections.translation: Why me ? Which of us ?

sections.label: Section 2.2.205c
sections.translation: I ’ faith , I don ’ t know . But if they were to do as you deserve , by my troth they ’ d hold you in hate , and treat you but badly .

sections.label: Section 2.2.207
sections.translation: Do leave off your abusive talking .

sections.label: Section 2.2.207b
sections.original: Quom ut digna es dico , bene , non male loquor .
sections.translation: Since I ’ m saying just as you I ’ m talking to deserve , properly , not abusively .
